entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop species studied for seed quality and longevity during germplasm regeneration

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: Environmental stress applied during flowering and grain filling to study its effect on seed longevity

entities.name: SEED LONGEVITY
entities.description: The duration for which seeds remain viable and capable of germination

entities.name: ICTP-8202
entities.description: A specific genotype of pearl millet used in the study

entities.name: ICTP-8203
entities.description: A specific genotype of pearl millet used in the study

entities.name: MBH-110
entities.description: A specific genotype of pearl millet used in the study

entities.name: OPEN POLLINATION
entities.description: A pollination method where pollen is transferred naturally, found to result in the greatest potential longevity

entities.name: SELFING
entities.description: A pollination method where individual panicles are enclosed in paper bags to prevent cross-pollination

entities.name: CLUSTER BAGGING
entities.description: A pollination method where panicles from 3–4 adjacent plants are enclosed in a paper bag

entities.name: AMBIENT CONDITIONS
entities.description: Storage environment characterized by temperatures of 20–40 °C and relative humidity of 30–80%

entities.name: GERMINATION
entities.description: The ability of the seed to sprout, used as the primary test for seed longevity

entities.name: ANALYSIS OF VARIANCE
entities.description: Statistical method used to determine the significance of water stress and pollination methods on seed longevity

entities.name: POTENTIAL SEED LONGEVITY
entities.description: The seed lot constant K i derived from the seed viability equation

entities.name: SEED VIABILITY EQUATION
entities.description: The mathematical model used to estimate the potential longevity of a seed lot

entities.name: GERMPLASM SEED PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of regenerating seeds to maintain genetic resources
